compositional_truth_check.p4_silver_guest: Present (Black Pot, shared with P2 remnants)

inventory_reconciliation.status: Partial loss detected. P2 (Mexican Mint) is no longer viable; only necrotic tissue remains.
inventory_reconciliation.anomalies: Presence of white perlite/debris on soil surface; P4 shows a single, isolated, upright leaf structure.

plant_audit.p1: Stable, succulent foliage visible in yellow pot.
plant_audit.p2: Necrotic, shriveled brown stem; no active growth.
plant_audit.p3: Pothos leaf shows progressive chlorosis and necrosis at the tip.
plant_audit.p4: Single, healthy-looking green leaf; appears to be the only active specimen in the black pot.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp/dark; no visible fungal blooms.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant organic debris outside of pots.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Minimal change; P3 leaf tip necrosis already present.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-1: Gradual decline in P3 leaf turgidity; P2 remains static in a state of decay.
temporal_deltas.current: P3 leaf shows further browning/desiccation compared to earliest image.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of mixed health. P1 and P4 are maintaining structural integrity. P3 is exhibiting signs of physiological stress, specifically tip necrosis, which has worsened over the 5-day observation period. P2 has suffered a total systemic loss, with no signs of recovery. The environment appears stable, but the decline in P3 suggests a potential issue with moisture regulation or light exposure.
